items.title: Hopes pinned on promising sequel to prevent a Whyalla wipeout

children.content: 'We've seen this movie before' is a phrase that sums up the way many people feel about the Whyalla steelworks' financial predicament, but the community is hopeful of a promising new sequel.

items.title: Helping 'big personality' marsupial recover after bushfires left them homeless

children.content: Artificial habitats are giving the Kangaroo Island dunnart a chance at recovery, after bushfires left them largely homeless.

image.altText: A small rodent in someone's hand, looking at the camera

items.title: Ex-police officer's messages did not amount to 'implied admissions' of rape, court told

children.content: A woman who says she was raped by a former South Australian police officer did not confront her alleged attacker "because she knew it wasn't true", a court has heard.

image.altText: Bare branches covering a large beige building in the background

items.title: Major winemaker considers moving business overseas if tax changes go through

children.content: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is considering leaving the country in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.

image.altText: Two people in flurescent vests walking through a vineyard

items.title: Major wine retailer puts vineyards up for sale amid industry-wide squeeze

children.content: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.

image.altText: Wine bottles on a bottle shop shelf.

items.title: Letter alleges 'unlawful conspiracy' to oust new APY Lands general manager

children.content: The new manager of a remote South Australian Aboriginal land corporation has alleged there is an "unlawful conspiracy" to oust him, but the board says his position has already been terminated.

image.altText: A man n a blue shirt is standing in shade outside a building with a sign that says Aṉangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara.

items.title: Buses, brekkie and circus skills getting kids through the school gate

children.content: Schools in the outback often struggle to maintain consistent attendance rates, but the Wilcannia community is going the extra mile to get kids into the classroom.

image.altText: Two smiling girls sitting on a bus.

items.title: Aussie diver called in for 'scary as hell' mission to rescue Laos villagers

children.content: A diver describes the "emotional moment" when he found a group of men alive after they were trapped for more than a week in a Laos cave, while a South Australian renowned for his ability to navigate tight spaces joins the rescue mission.

gallery.altText: A man in a suit and orange helmet climbing backwards in a cave.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a suit and orange helmet climbing backwards in a cave.

items.title: Adelaide man to face court over alleged fuel drive-offs using trucks

children.content: A man has been charged for allegedly using trucks in serial petrol drive-offs north of Adelaide, with police estimating the cost to be more than $8,000.

image.altText: A old truck parked on grass in a backyard next to a fence

items.title: Waste company fined $1.1m over 2014 double-fatal Adelaide truck crash

children.content: Almost 12 years after a Cleanaway truck killed two people and injured two others, the company has been sentenced in South Australia's Supreme Court.

image.altText: A person in full coveralls spraying water on the road next to a sewage tanker truck on its side

items.title: After escaping sexual slavery, Yazidis re-traumatised by return of 'ISIS brides'

children.content: The return of several 'ISIS brides' from Syria has been re-traumatising for Australian Yazidis, many of whom escaped sexual slavery at the hands of the terrorist organisation.

image.altText: A portrait shot of Amera Ali.

items.title: No new evidence in Gus Lamont search, but police 'won't give up'

children.content: A renewed search for missing four-year-old boy Gus Lamont has failed to uncover additional evidence, police reveal.

image.altText: An SA Police officer at Oak Park Station.

items.title: Man joins brother and late mother's reconciliation journey after discovering he is Indigenous

children.content: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

gallery.altText: Two men with arms on each other's shoulders looking at a fire

thumbnail.altText: Two men with arms on each other's shoulders looking at a fire

items.title: 'Lost opportunity' to help air force serviceman before he died, inquest finds

children.content: A coroner has found that the death of an RAAF serviceman could have been prevented, including when he left a military health centre during a mental health crisis.
Warning: This story contains discussion of suicide and mental health issues.

image.altText: The Coroners Court sign in Adelaide.

items.title: Modelling suggests 'second wave' of algal bloom in Spencer Gulf

children.content: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

image.altText: A man sits at a table near the beach with a laptop.

items.title: 'Heart ripped out on repeat': Auskick parents say they have been failed by AFL

children.content: The ABC has spoken to several families who say they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.

items.title: Mount Isa mum calls for more support after birth of premature twins

children.content: Mount Isa mother Kristen Curd spent four months in Townsville after giving birth to premature twin boys, and says the hardest part began when they finally came home.

image.altText: A mother holding her twin boys, standing infront of a Royal Flying Doctor Service aeroplane.

items.title: Heat during abnormally warm May for eastern states could be here to stay

children.content: Australia's east is coming to the end of an abnormally warm May, and the heat could be here to stay.

image.altText: an empty bench overlooks the ocean at Clifton Springs in victoria

items.title: 'Perfect storm' for corruption: IOC petition targets Brisbane's 2032 Olympic stadium plan

children.content: Opponents of Brisbane's centrepiece Olympic stadium take their concerns directly to the IOC's headquarters in Switzerland as a critical deadline looms.

items.title: Canadian man accused of selling chemical online admits aiding suicide

children.content: In 2023, Interpol alerted Australian police that packages sold by the man, which contained a lethal substance, were sent to Australia, prompting authorities to check on the welfare of recipients.

image.altText: A man in glasses

items.title: First of five survivors rescued from flooded Laos cave

children.content: Rescue divers in Laos have safely evacuated the first of five local villagers who had been trapped in a cave for more than a week by floodwaters.

image.altText: A video grab showing a rescuer evacuating the first of five villagers trapped in a cave.

items.title: The simple, ordinary joy of Kylie Minogue

children.content: In the end, a woman who's had wins and losses, terrors and triumphs, and who regularly goes back to stay with family in suburban Melbourne, has become a music legend of her time, and it's not hard to figure out why.

gallery.altText: pop star Kylie Minogue sits in a dressing gown on a deck chair smiling pensively.

thumbnail.altText: pop star Kylie Minogue sits in a dressing gown on a deck chair smiling pensively.

items.title: How women over 60 are still being made the butt of the joke in film

children.content: Films are four times more likely to feature a talking animal lead than a woman over 60. These charts explain the state of film for older women.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of John Farnham, 60s pop and where to live on the Moon

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: The nine books our critics couldn't put down in May

children.content: Sweet teen love, gay football stars, alien abductions and a murderous PhD student bent on revenge — this month's best new releases have it all.

items.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during test

children.content: Blue Origin says all personnel are accounted for after experiencing an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

items.title: How IVF could spell doom for feral rabbits

children.content: Scientists are using rabbit IVF and gene-editing technology to crash Australia's feral rabbit population by spreading infertility through female rabbits.

image.altText: A brown rabbit with a white tail leaps into the air above green grass.

items.title: Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president

children.content: Former prime minister Tony Abbott says he is ready to serve the Liberal Party in its time of 'existential crisis' upon his return to the political stage.

children.content: The website features a space-inspired background but its real purpose is to highlight the work of ICE.

items.title: Eight students arrested over deadly girls' school fire in Kenya

children.content: The fire, at the Utumishi Girls' Academy Senior School in Gilgil in west-central Kenya, broke out in the early hours of Thursday morning and also injured 79 students.

items.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?

children.content: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
You might have seen AI generated memes of Albanese on social media following the announcement of the federal budget.
Whether or not those are legal comes down to what the post is actually communicating to the people seeing it online.
